description: Build a browser-native TypeScript port of BBC Master 128 Elite (Acornsoft) with jsbeeb-backed deterministic checks and captured visual evidence for fidelity verification.

userStories.title: Record the reference contract and phase-1 non-goals
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want the canonical reference variant and non-goals documented up front so that every later implementation task targets the same correctness contract.
acceptanceCriteria: `docs/elite-typescript-port-step-by-step.md` and `docs/tasks/reference-contract.md` are created or updated as the port runbook and reference contract
The reference contract records `BBC Master 128 (DFS)` in `jsbeeb`, the SNG47 disc, the canonical local corpus, the non-canonical exploratory artifact, and the explicit phase-1 non-goals
The runbook states that implementation work starts only after the reference variant and non-goals are written down

userStories.title: Add root TypeScript, Vite, and Vitest toolchain config
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want the repository wired for TypeScript, Vite, and Vitest so that browser code and verification code have a stable build and test foundation.
acceptanceCriteria: The root Node engine or version note aligns with `tools/jsbeeb/package.json` unless an explicit divergence is documented

userStories.title: Scaffold browser entry, game shell, and path aliases
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want the browser entrypoint and module layout scaffolded so that runtime code, platform adapters, and UI code have clear homes before subsystem work begins.
acceptanceCriteria: TypeScript path aliases are defined for `src/core`, `src/platform`, and `src/ui`
The browser app renders a visible shell placeholder and test code can import through the configured aliases
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Boot the canonical jsbeeb reference session
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want a reliable reference-session bootstrap so that every fixture and comparison starts from the same machine contract.
acceptanceCriteria: The harness boots the SNG47 disc on `BBC Master 128 (DFS)` and exposes a stable session contract for later helpers
Reference-harness tests prove the session boots successfully without modifying `tools/jsbeeb` internals for the port runtime

userStories.title: Add stepping, breakpoint, memory, and input helpers to the reference harness
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want deterministic stepping and memory helpers so that fixture extraction can drive the reference machine precisely.
acceptanceCriteria: `scripts/reference/breakpoints.ts` and supporting helpers expose run-for-cycle, run-until-address, memory read, memory write, and key input operations
The harness can stop on breakpoints and mutate memory deterministically during a booted session
Reference-harness tests cover breakpoint hits, cycle stepping, memory IO, and key input helpers

userStories.title: Add text capture, screenshot, and snapshot helpers to the reference harness
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want capture and restore helpers on the harness so that later stories can produce repeatable text, image, and state evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: `scripts/reference/snapshot.ts` and supporting helpers expose text capture, screenshot capture, and snapshot/restore operations
A booted reference session can capture text output, save a snapshot, restore it, and continue deterministically
Reference-harness tests cover screenshot capture, text capture, and snapshot round-tripping

userStories.title: Define reference and visual fixture manifests
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want fixture conventions documented before subsystem work starts so that every later module proves correctness the same way.
acceptanceCriteria: Fixture locations are defined for `rng`, `galaxy`, `market`, `text`, `movement`, `tactics`, `commander`, `screens`, and `scenes`
The manifest and READMEs require every fixture to record machine/disc, source routine context, setup method, exact inputs, exact expected outputs or image, and the capture script used

userStories.title: Add a deterministic fixture extraction CLI
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want a reusable extraction script so that deterministic reference fixtures can be added consistently as subsystems are ported.
acceptanceCriteria: The CLI writes fixture records that match the reference manifest contract, including setup metadata and capture-script provenance
At least one deterministic extraction path is implemented and exercised by tests

userStories.title: Add a scene capture CLI for visual fixtures
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want a dedicated scene-capture tool so that later renderer work can compare bounded browser scenes against captured reference images.
acceptanceCriteria: The CLI captures named scenes into `tests/fixtures/visual/` using the visual fixture conventions
At least one scene capture path is implemented and exercised by tests or a scripted smoke check

userStories.title: Implement `u8`, `u16`, and bitwise helpers
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want explicit fixed-width byte helpers so that JavaScript number behavior does not leak into ported gameplay logic.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/core/arithmetic/u8.ts`, `src/core/arithmetic/u16.ts`, `src/core/arithmetic/bitwise.ts`, and matching unit tests are created
Helpers cover masking, wraparound, carry, borrow, shifts, and rotates needed by the first deterministic subsystems
Arithmetic tests prove reference-relevant wraparound and carry behavior

userStories.title: Implement sign-magnitude and fixed-width helpers
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want the signed-byte conventions modeled explicitly so that movement and coordinate logic can preserve Elite's original storage semantics.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/core/arithmetic/sign-magnitude.ts`, `src/core/arithmetic/fixed-width.ts`, and matching unit tests are created
Helpers support sign-magnitude encode/decode plus small readability wrappers used by simulation code
Unit tests cover sign-magnitude round-tripping and any shared fixed-width helpers added for later subsystems

userStories.title: Model ZP, WP, and UP workspace state
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want named workspace structures instead of opaque byte arrays so that later subsystem ports can work with readable typed state.
acceptanceCriteria: Each workspace exposes named fields and lossless conversion to and from the byte layouts needed by fixtures
Unit tests round-trip workspace state through the serializer without losing byte fidelity

userStories.title: Model `K%` and `INWK` ship byte layouts
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want ship-block structures modeled explicitly so that ship logic can preserve original semantics without exposing raw memory blobs as the main API.
acceptanceCriteria: The ship-state modules expose named fields while preserving lossless conversion to and from fixture byte layouts
Unit tests round-trip ship block and `INWK` state without byte drift

userStories.title: Add commander state and shared serialization helpers
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want commander state and generic serialization primitives in place so that save data and stateful systems can build on a consistent typed surface.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/core/state/commander/commander-state.ts`, `src/core/state/serialization/`, and matching unit tests are created
Shared serializers support lossless conversion for commander data and the first ported subsystems that depend on byte layouts
Unit tests cover serializer round-trips and commander-state round-trips

userStories.title: Port ship blueprints and lookup tables
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want the core lookup data represented as typed modules so that simulation code can depend on stable data without assembly-era runtime structures.
acceptanceCriteria: Ship blueprint data and lookup-table content needed by early simulation modules match extracted reference fixtures
Tests verify the typed modules against captured reference data

userStories.title: Port market and text token data tables
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want market tables and token tables in typed modules so that world generation and text systems can consume reference-matched data.
acceptanceCriteria: The market tables and token tables match extracted reference fixtures for representative captured cases
Tests verify market and token data against fixture snapshots

userStories.title: Add dashboard data and asset module scaffold
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want presentation constants and asset modules scaffolded so that renderer stories can depend on typed assets instead of ad hoc embedded data.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard or font-related presentation data needed by the initial browser shell is represented as typed modules or asset metadata
Tests prove the required dashboard asset modules resolve and load through the new typed surface

userStories.title: Port RNG core and galaxy seed helpers
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want Elite's deterministic random state modeled first so that later world-generation modules can reproduce reference outputs exactly.
acceptanceCriteria: Known RNG sequences and seed transitions match captured reference fixtures exactly
Reference tests cover the deterministic seed and RNG helpers used by later world-generation code

userStories.title: Port galaxy system generation
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want galaxy system generation ported next so that system records can be compared against the reference build before map and travel features depend on them.
acceptanceCriteria: Known system coordinates, economy, government, tech level, population, and productivity values match captured reference fixtures
Reference tests verify deterministic system generation for representative seeds and galaxies

userStories.title: Port system-name generation
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want system names generated from typed TypeScript logic so that canonical names and description inputs no longer depend on the original runtime layout.
acceptanceCriteria: Known system names for representative captured seeds match the reference fixtures exactly
Tests verify the ported name-generation logic against the recorded reference corpus

userStories.title: Port market generation
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want market-generation logic ported independently so that economy systems can build on exact prices and availabilities for captured systems.
acceptanceCriteria: Prices and availabilities for representative captured systems match the reference fixtures exactly
Reference tests cover deterministic market generation for known seeds and systems

userStories.title: Implement vector math and arctan helpers
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want the low-level presentation math isolated first so that later projection and rendering code can depend on deterministic primitives.
acceptanceCriteria: Vector operations and angle helpers match captured reference outputs for curated inputs
Tests verify the math helpers before any scene renderer depends on them

userStories.title: Implement projection helpers
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want projection behavior verified in isolation so that full renderers can build on reference-matched screen transforms.
acceptanceCriteria: Projection helpers match captured reference outputs for curated test scenes before any full-scene renderer is added
Reference tests verify deterministic projection outputs for the chosen fixture set

userStories.title: Implement pixel, line, and circle drawing primitives
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want raster primitives proven against reference fixtures so that later scene renderers inherit stable low-level drawing behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Pixel plotting, line rasterization, and circle drawing match captured reference outputs for curated primitive fixtures
Reference tests verify isolated raster outputs before ship or dashboard renderers are added

userStories.title: Model local-universe and scanner state
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want the local-universe state represented explicitly so that movement, tactics, and scanner logic can share typed runtime structures.
acceptanceCriteria: The local-universe and scanner structures can represent all bytes and fields needed by the first captured movement scenarios
Tests prove the state objects serialize or initialize correctly for fixture-backed scenarios

userStories.title: Port ship spawn and orientation transforms
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want spawn and orientation logic isolated so that ship-placement behavior can be verified before full movement updates run.
acceptanceCriteria: Captured spawn and orientation cases match the reference harness outputs for representative scenarios
Tests verify deterministic orientation transforms before per-tick ship movement depends on them

userStories.title: Port ship movement and local-bubble updates
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want local ship motion ported against fixtures so that the runtime can maintain the in-flight bubble with reference semantics.
acceptanceCriteria: Captured local-bubble movement scenarios match the reference harness for ship updates and bubble-state changes
Reference tests cover representative travel and ordinary in-flight update cases

userStories.title: Port tactical decision rules
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want tactical state transitions ported independently so that combat behavior can be checked before weapon and collision rules are layered on top.
acceptanceCriteria: Representative tactical decision scenarios match captured reference state transitions and target-selection outcomes
Reference tests cover the curated tactics fixtures needed by later combat stories

userStories.title: Port laser, missile, and collision resolution
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want the immediate combat rules ported in one focused story so that hit resolution and collision outcomes can be checked against captured evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: Representative combat scenarios match captured reference state transitions, weapon outcomes, and collision results
Reference tests cover at least one laser, missile, and collision fixture path

userStories.title: Port docking checks and docking computer logic
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want docking behavior isolated so that eligibility checks and auto-docking flow can be verified before full game-loop integration.
acceptanceCriteria: Representative docking scenarios match captured reference eligibility checks and docking-computer behavior
Reference tests cover the curated docking fixtures selected for phase 1

userStories.title: Port cargo scooping logic
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want scooping rules ported separately so that cargo-state transitions can be checked without bundling them into larger flight-loop changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Representative scooping scenarios match captured reference cargo and ship-state transitions
Tests verify the scooping logic against the selected fixture set

userStories.title: Implement flight context and per-tick orchestration
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want the main flight loop expressed as readable TypeScript modules so that the runtime can replace the reference orchestration path without preserving opaque control flow.
acceptanceCriteria: The flight loop preserves observable ordering and effects for the subsystems already ported while remaining readable and modular
Unit or integration tests exercise the flight-step orchestration surface before reference-scenario comparisons are added

userStories.title: Add curated main flight loop reference scenarios
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want stepped flight-loop comparisons against jsbeeb so that the integrated in-flight behavior is proven on captured scenarios rather than by code inspection.
acceptanceCriteria: Stepped scenarios covering combat, docking, planet approach, and ordinary travel compare the TypeScript loop against captured jsbeeb evidence
The reference scenarios fail on observable mismatches in ordering or state transitions

userStories.title: Implement main game-loop scheduler and view-state orchestration
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want the top-level loop and scheduler ported before docked-flow edge cases so that flight and view-state transitions have a clear TypeScript home.
acceptanceCriteria: The top-level loop can enter play and continue scheduler ordering for the subsystems already ported
Tests cover the scheduler and view-state orchestration surface before docked-flow integration is added

userStories.title: Add docked-flow transitions and spawn-rule integration
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want docked transitions and event spawning verified after the game-loop scaffold exists so that top-level flow stays ordered like the reference build.
acceptanceCriteria: Captured scenarios prove the loop can transition between in-flight and docked flows while keeping event-spawn ordering aligned with the reference build
Reference tests fail on mismatched docked-flow transitions or spawn ordering

userStories.title: Port trade, equipment, and status logic
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want the primary non-flight economy rules ported together so that docked gameplay can manipulate cargo, equipment, and commander status with reference-backed behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Representative trade calculations, equipment effects, and status outputs match captured reference data
Reference tests cover the curated docked-economy fixture set

userStories.title: Port galaxy map and hyperspace logic
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want navigation and hyperspace behavior ported independently so that travel transitions can be validated before mission or shell integration relies on them.
acceptanceCriteria: Representative route-selection and hyperspace-transition outputs match captured reference fixtures
Tests cover the navigation and hyperspace fixture cases selected for phase 1

userStories.title: Port mission-state logic
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want mission progression modeled separately so that scripted state transitions can be checked without bundling them into broader docked-flow work.
acceptanceCriteria: Representative mission progression scenarios match captured reference data for the chosen mission fixtures
Reference tests cover mission-state transitions and stored mission data for the curated cases

userStories.title: Port commander save format and save-load flows
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want commander persistence ported before browser storage integration so that save semantics can be verified separately from platform adapters.
acceptanceCriteria: Commander save/load round trips and representative captured commander fields match the chosen reference rules
Reference tests cover save-format serialization and load behavior for the curated fixtures

userStories.title: Port token expansion and text formatting
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want text formatting separated from procedural generation so that token-driven UI strings can be validated before goat-soup logic is added.
acceptanceCriteria: Representative market, system, and scripted token expansion outputs match captured reference fixtures
Tests verify token expansion and formatting behavior for the selected text cases

userStories.title: Port goat-soup procedural text generation
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want generated descriptive text ported in a dedicated story so that goat-soup behavior can be compared against representative reference output independently.
acceptanceCriteria: Representative generated descriptions match captured reference fixtures for the selected seeds and contexts
Reference tests cover the goat-soup fixture set without relying on screen-memory-era printing logic

userStories.title: Implement the Canvas2D renderer boundary and framebuffer
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want a browser rendering adapter boundary before scene renderers are added so that the first implementation is playable while remaining replaceable by future backends.
acceptanceCriteria: The renderer boundary keeps browser-specific drawing concerns separate from deterministic core presentation logic
A browser shell can present framebuffer output through the Canvas2D adapter
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Implement ship, planet, sun, and starfield renderers
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want the main space-scene renderers split into one focused story so that the largest visible scene elements can be checked against captured reference images.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/core/presentation/renderers/ship-renderer.ts`, `src/core/presentation/renderers/planet-renderer.ts`, `src/core/presentation/renderers/sun-renderer.ts`, `src/core/presentation/renderers/starfield-renderer.ts`, and matching visual tests are created
Bounded ship, planet, sun, and starfield scenes meet the chosen pixel or perceptual regression thresholds against captured reference images
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Implement scanner, dashboard, and sights renderers
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want cockpit-oriented renderers ported in a separate story so that HUD fidelity can be checked independently from world-scene rendering.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/core/presentation/renderers/scanner-renderer.ts`, `src/core/presentation/renderers/dashboard-renderer.ts`, `src/core/presentation/renderers/sights-renderer.ts`, and matching visual tests are created
Bounded scanner, dashboard, and sights scenes meet the chosen pixel or perceptual regression thresholds against captured reference images
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add visual regression coverage for curated render scenes
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want the renderer stories backed by stable regression coverage so that future changes cannot silently drift away from the captured reference scenes.
acceptanceCriteria: `tests/visual/` includes curated regression tests for ships, sun, planet, starfield, scanner, and dashboard scenes
Regression thresholds and fixture selection are documented in the visual test suite for the chosen scenes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Implement browser keyboard and joystick adapters
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want input adapters separated from the core simulation so that browser controls can be verified without coupling them to flight logic internals.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/platform/browser/input/keyboard.ts`, `src/platform/browser/input/joystick.ts`, and matching integration tests are created
Keyboard controls and optional joystick input are translated into the shell input contract with stable bindings
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Implement browser audio and commander storage adapters
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want audio and persistence adapters isolated from core gameplay so that browser-specific side effects can be tested and replaced independently.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/platform/browser/audio/audio-engine.ts`, `src/platform/browser/storage/commander-storage.ts`, and matching integration tests are created
Audio cues and commander persistence work through browser adapters without leaking browser APIs into deterministic core modules
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Integrate the game shell with HUD and screen modules
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want the browser shell to compose the runtime adapters and UI modules so that the port becomes a playable application instead of disconnected subsystems.
acceptanceCriteria: The game shell wires input, audio, storage, rendering, and view-state adapters into a playable browser flow
Integration tests cover the shell contract for the initial playable paths
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Implement bootstrap, loading, and title screens
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want the startup flow split from in-game screens so that the product can boot into a usable title path without reproducing original loader choreography.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/app/bootstrap.ts`, `src/ui/screens/title-screen.ts`, `src/ui/screens/loading-screen.ts`, and matching integration tests are created
The browser build boots through loading into a usable title flow while explicitly omitting copy-protection or disc-loader choreography that is out of scope
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Implement flight and docked screen transitions
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want the core user-facing screen transitions verified separately so that title, flight, and docked paths behave like one coherent product.
acceptanceCriteria: The browser build can enter gameplay and return cleanly between title, flight, and docked paths for the supported phase-1 flows
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Expand deterministic end-to-end reference acceptance tests
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want broad automated evidence for reference-backed gameplay flows so that the port's core simulation is defended against regression across integrated systems.
acceptanceCriteria: `tests/reference/acceptance/` is created and populated with deterministic acceptance coverage
Acceptance flows cover the curated phase-1 scenarios for launch, docking, trade, hyperspace, combat, death, and save/load using fixture-backed checks

userStories.title: Expand visual acceptance coverage and playtest documentation
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want visual evidence and human-check guidance maintained together so that fidelity is proven both mechanically and experientially.
acceptanceCriteria: Visual acceptance covers the curated dashboard, scanner, ship, sun, planet, title, and loading scenes used for phase-1 review
The docs record input feel, movement feel, docking feel, combat feel, audio feel, and every known fidelity gap with a clear fix, acceptance, or follow-up state
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add browser build packaging and CI workflows
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want repeatable build and CI automation so that another engineer can verify the browser port without reconstructing the workflow by hand.
acceptanceCriteria: Browser build output configuration and CI scripts are created or updated at the repository root
The project exposes one command for local development, one for the full test suite, one for reference extraction refresh, and one for production build
CI runs the relevant build and test commands successfully

userStories.title: Document release, legal, and verification workflow
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want the release and verification docs finalized so that another engineer can understand the corpus, constraints, and validation approach behind the browser port.
acceptanceCriteria: Root `README.md` and `docs/tasks/release-checklist.md` are created or updated
The documentation explains the reference corpus, legal constraints, verification harness, and how to build, test, and run the browser port
Another engineer can follow the documented commands and understand the relationship between the TypeScript port and the reference corpus
